commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Discord-specific message-tool-only prompt guidance favoring reactions or silence for stale or low-value chatter, plus system-prompt assertions.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +15. Total +19 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: the proposed predicate cannot distinguish Discord direct messages from group/thread turns, while current runtime and tests track those contexts separately. The actual model response effect has not been demonstrated live.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR has concrete repairable defects, but its protected `maintainer` label requires explicit human handling and the missing real-environment proof cannot be supplied by an automated repair worker.

target.title: fix(agent): dampen Discord stale thread replies
target.body:
## Summary
- add Discord-specific message-tool-only guidance that a mention does not require a visible reply
- steer stale threads, jokes, lightweight acknowledgements, and low-value chatter toward reaction or silence
- cover the prompt behavior with system prompt assertions

## Diagnosis
This was not a Twilio delivery bug. In a Discord thread where final assistant text is private, the runtime correctly exposed `message(action=send)` as the visible reply path. The agent prompt did not make the channel-noise tradeoff explicit enough, so a mention in an old thread got treated as reason to post instead of react/silence.

## Tests
- `pnpm vitest run src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts` *(blocked: this workspace clone has no `node_modules`; Vitest cannot resolve `vitest/config`)*
- `git diff --check --cached` before commit
target.author: steipete
